sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Your next can of Coke might have a Minnesota star on it
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is launching a series of collectible mini cans featuring unique designs for each of the 50 states, Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia to celebrate America's 250th birthday. These "America250" cans, which include regular Coke, Coke Zero, and Diet Coke, will be available at major retailers starting this month. The campaign also includes interactive experiences, prizes like a new Jeep, and additional commemorative packaging on other Coca-Cola brands, alongside a commitment to 250,000 volunteer hours and a public art initiative.

feed.title: Microsoft Halts Carbon Credit Purchases: Market Impact & Future - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Microsoft has reportedly paused its carbon removal credit purchases for 2026, citing financial considerations, despite being the dominant investor in this market. While some project developers were informed, a company spokesperson denied an indefinite suspension, and some existing contractors had not received official notification. This development occurs as Microsoft's own greenhouse gas emissions have substantially risen due to data center investments, even as it maintains a long-term goal of carbon negativity.
